decks.notes: This deck focuses on overwhelming your opponent with fast, aggressive creatures and utilizing mana acceleration from creatures like Llanowar Elves to quickly ramp into powerful threats like Mossborn Hydra. The key strategy is to establish a wide board presence early on with cards like Badgermole Cub and Sazh's Chocobo, while also utilizing cards like Earthbender Ascension to benefit from having a board full of creatures. By keeping the pressure on your opponent and quickly building up your board, you aim to overwhelm them before they can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to be aggressive and apply pressure on the opponent with a focus on creatures with abilities that can deal damage quickly. By utilizing cards like Badgermole Cub, Mossborn Hydra, and Llanowar Elves, the deck aims to build a strong board presence early on. Support cards like Snakeskin Veil and Earthbender Ascension help protect and enhance the creatures while Escape Tunnel and Fabled Passage provide mana fixing and card draw. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient creatures and secure victory through aggressive plays.

This aggressive Dimir deck focuses on quick, efficient creature plays and disruptive spells to control the tempo of the game. By using cards like Spyglass Siren and Floodpits Drowner to pressure the opponent's life total while utilizing spells like Requiting Hex and Shoot the Sheriff to hinder their game plan, this deck seeks to establish board presence early and maintain pressure throughout the match. With a mix of evasive threats and removal, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly and efficiently.

The deck focuses on using a combination of control spells and card advantage to outlast and outvalue the opponent. By utilizing a variety of removal spells, card draw effects, and counterspells, the player aims to control the game and dictate the pace. The deck also includes utility creatures and powerful finishers to close out the game. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure consistent access to all three colors of mana required for the spells in the deck. Overall, the strategy revolves around managing threats and resources effectively to outplay the opponent.

This deck focuses on aggressive play using a combination of powerful creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. With cards like Deceit, Floodpits Drowner, and Spyglass Siren providing early threats, followed by Superior Spider-Man and other strong creatures to maintain pressure. Utilizing cards like Requiting Hex and Bitter Triumph to disrupt the opponent's plans, while Kaito, Bane of Nightmares serves as a finisher. The deck also includes some control elements with Dream Beavers and Shoot the Sheriff. Overall, the strategy is to maintain a fast and aggressive tempo to secure victory.

The main objective of this deck is to control the game by using a variety of spells and abilities to manipulate the board state and disrupt your opponent's strategy. With cards like Gran-Gran and Combustion Technique, you can generate card advantage and deal damage to your opponent's creatures. Monument to Endurance and Abandon Attachments help you maintain card advantage and control the tempo of the game. By utilizing counterspells like Spell Snare and Iroh's Demonstration, you can prevent your opponent from resolving key spells, while also utilizing powerful card draw spells like Accumulate Wisdom and Artist's Talent to dig through your deck and find your win conditions.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with creatures through card draw, discarding, and sacrificing, then reanimating them onto the battlefield for powerful combos and synergies. It utilizes a mix of creature abilities, removal spells, and counter magic to control the board while setting up the game-winning combination of cards. The key strategy is to efficiently reanimate impactful creatures while disrupting the opponent's game plan, ultimately overwhelming them with a strong board presence and unique abilities.

The primary focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bring them back onto the battlefield using reanimation spells. By utilizing cards like Deceit and Requiting Hex to discard key creatures, the deck can set up explosive combos with cards like Doomsday Excruciator and Harvester of Misery. The deck also includes control elements like Bitter Triumph and Deadly Cover-Up to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its own win conditions. The mana base is designed to support both black and blue spells, ensuring consistency in executing the reanimation plan.

The deck focuses on a fast-paced aggressive strategy with a mix of flying creatures and powerful spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes a combination of control elements and efficient creatures to maintain board presence and apply pressure. The key concept revolves around utilizing evasion to deal damage quickly while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with removal spells and tempo plays. The deck aims to close out the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize, utilizing a mix of creatures and spells to maintain aggression throughout the match.

The deck focuses on utilizing cards that synergize well together to create a powerful combo using Bant Airbending creatures like Aang and Appa, supported by cards like Bramble Familiar and Doc Aurlock. The deck includes cards for ramp like Llanowar Elves and Interdimensional Web Watch, as well as removal options like Michelangelo's Technique and Seam Rip. The mana base is designed to support the color requirements of the deck, with lands like Breeding Pool, Hallowed Fountain, and Temple Garden. The strategy revolves around building a strong board presence and overwhelming the opponent with a well-timed combo.

This deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and efficient combat tricks. By playing small, efficient creatures like Hired Claw and Stadium Headliner, backed up by powerful combat spells like Boros Charm and The Last Ronin's Technique, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. The inclusion of cards like Warleader's Call and Voice of Victory helps to maintain card advantage and board presence, ensuring that the deck can continue to apply pressure until victory is achieved.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying powerful dragon creatures to overwhelm the opponent's defenses. With a mix of strong creatures like Magmatic Hellkite and Nova Hellkite, along with support spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Helix, the deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain board control throughout the game. Cards like Clarion Conqueror and Voice of Victory help boost the team's power and protect against removal, while Sarkhan, Dragon Ascendant can provide additional value and aggression. This deck aims to close out games swiftly with a relentless assault of flying dragons.

This mono green aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures and using them to overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards like Badgermole Cub and Llanowar Elves to accelerate mana production, the deck aims to consistently play threats and apply pressure. Earthbender Ascension and Sapling Nursery provide additional support by generating tokens and buffing creatures. Escape Tunnel and Fabled Passage help ensure smooth mana development, while cards like Esper Origins and Mightform Harmonizer offer versatility and flexibility in combat situations. The goal is to establish a strong board presence and close out the game swiftly.

This deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a fast and aggressive approach using efficient creature drops and powerful support spells. With a strong mana base and a mix of low-cost and mid-range creatures, it aims to quickly build a board presence and apply pressure to the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Earthbender Ascension and Sapling Nursery enable the player to further accelerate their board development, while Escape Tunnel and Fabled Passage provide additional flexibility and options for mana fixing. Ultimately, the goal is to outpace the opponent and secure victories through early and consistent aggression.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent with aggressive creatures and spells, using cards that can deal damage quickly and efficiently. This deck focuses on maintaining board presence and tempo, utilizing cards like Flamebraider and Roaming Throne to consistently apply pressure. Additionally, cards like Wistfulness and Vibrance provide support for your creatures and help maintain card advantage. With a mix of combat tricks and removal spells like Bounce Off and Sunderflock, this deck aims to control the battlefield and secure a victory through aggression.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent with fast, aggressive creatures that have synergistic abilities. By playing creatures like Badgermole Cub, Gene Pollinator, and Quantum Riddler early on, you can quickly build a threatening board presence. The inclusion of cards like Nature's Rhythm and Multiversal Passage allow for mana fixing and ramping, ensuring you can play your higher-cost creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth and Leatherhead, Swamp Stalker. Overall, the deck aims to establish a dominant board state and close out the game quickly with powerful finishers like Marang River Regent and Bounce Off.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells in order to quickly reduce their life total to zero. The deck features a mix of powerful creatures like Ashling, Rekindled and Flamebraider, along with efficient burn spells like Burst Lightning and Ill-Timed Explosion. The deck also includes cards like Vibrance and Wistfulness to support the aggressive strategy by providing additional card advantage and resource management. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and close out games quickly before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on early aggression and tempo control, utilizing low-cost creatures like Aang, Swift Savior and Aven Interrupter to quickly pressure the opponent. The inclusion of disruptive spells like Spell Snare and Get Lost helps to maintain board dominance while card draw effects such as Enduring Curiosity and No More Lies ensure consistent resources. High Noon and Seam Rip provide additional removal options, while powerful finishers like Ajani, Outland Chaperone and Avatar's Wrath can quickly close out games. The mana base is optimized for consistency and allows for flexibility in casting spells of both colors.

The deck focuses on creating a powerful combo using Aang, Swift Savior, and other support creatures like Appa and Badgermole Cub to control the board and overwhelm the opponent. Doc Aurlock provides card advantage, while Interdimensional Web Watch and Seam Rip disrupt the opponent's strategy. Airbender Ascension enhances the deck's abilities, and Wistfulness provides additional utility. The mana base includes a mix of lands to ensure consistent access to all colors needed for the combo. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and outmaneuver the opponent for victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures with evasion abilities to quickly deal damage to the opponent. Using a mix of flying and unblockable creatures, the deck aims to pressure the opponent early and often. The inclusion of cards like Enduring Curiosity and Tishana's Tidebinder allows for card draw and tempo advantage. Bitter Triumph and Requiting Hex provide removal and disruption options, while Kaito, Bane of Nightmares acts as a finisher. The deck also includes mana fixing and protection with Gloomlake Verge, Soulstone Sanctuary, and Multiversal Passage to ensure consistency in casting its spells.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. The goal is to control the board while dealing damage consistently, using cards like Dream Beavers and Floodpits Drowner to pressure the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Spyglass Siren and Super Shredder to disrupt the opponent's strategy and protect your own creatures. Overall, the key concept of this Dimir Aggro deck is to maintain a strong offensive presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

This Esper Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a mix of powerful creatures like Aang, Swift Savior and Floodpits Drowner, while also disrupting their strategy with cards like Get Lost and Spell Snare. The deck is rounded out with a solid mana base and support cards like High Noon and Seam Rip to maintain pressure on the opponent. Wan Shi Tong, Librarian and Voice of Victory provide additional value and synergy with the rest of the deck, allowing for a fast and aggressive playstyle.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of card advantage, removal, and versatile spells. The deck aims to maintain board control while using cards like Gran-Gran and Monument to Endurance to generate card advantage and sustain itself. Abandon Attachments and Accumulate Wisdom help filter through the deck to find necessary cards, while Combustion Technique and Firebending Lesson serve as removal spells. Additionally, cards like It'll Quench Ya! and Boomerang Basics offer additional forms of control. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent through resource management and precise spell usage.

The main idea behind this deck is to overwhelm your opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle, utilizing the combination of blue and red cards to quickly deal damage. By utilizing low-cost creatures like Elusive Otter and Slickshot Show-Off, alongside instant spells like Burst Lightning and Opt, you can maintain board control and pressure your opponent. Additionally, cards like Drake Hatcher and Stormchaser's Talent provide added versatility and power to your attacks. With a focus on speed and efficiency, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver your opponent to secure victory.

The deck focuses on using low-cost aggressive creatures like Elusive Otter and Slickshot Show-Off to quickly apply pressure on the opponent. Burst Lightning and Stormchaser's Talent provide additional direct damage to clear the way for attacks. The spells Opt, Sleight of Hand, and Stock Up help the player dig for more threats or answers. The deck also utilizes cards like Into the Flood Maw and Secret Identity for tempo swings and disruption. The mana base includes both basic Islands and dual lands like Riverpyre Verge and Spirebluff Canal for consistency in casting spells.

The main concept of this deck revolves around utilizing quick and efficient creatures like Elusive Otter and Slickshot Show-Off to deal damage to the opponent early on. Burst Lightning and Stormchaser's Talent provide additional direct damage spells to complement the creature aggression. Opt, Sleight of Hand, and Stock Up help to maintain card advantage and ensure smooth draws. The deck also includes some control elements like Boomerang Basics and Secret Identity for disruption. The mana base is focused on generating blue and red mana efficiently to support the aggressive playstyle.

This deck focuses on assembling the Omniscience Combo, using cards like Kona, Rescue Beastie and Marang River Regent to generate card advantage and control the board. The deck also includes key pieces like Roiling Dragonstorm and Uthros, Titanic Godcore to quickly close out the game once Omniscience is in play. With a mix of disruption and card draw spells, the deck aims to set up its combo pieces efficiently and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing a combination of efficient creatures and spells to apply constant pressure on the opponent, aiming to quickly overwhelm them before they can stabilize. By utilizing cards like Badgermole Cub, Gene Pollinator, and Llanowar Elves to establish an early board presence, followed by powerful finishers like Craterhoof Behemoth, the deck aims to consistently push for damage and maintain board control. The inclusion of cards like Quantum Riddler and Ouroboroid also provide additional disruption and utility to support the aggro strategy.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bringing them back to the battlefield for a devastating combo. It includes cards that allow for graveyard manipulation, protection, and card draw to support the reanimation strategy. The key is to efficiently set up the graveyard and carefully choose the right moment to bring back key creatures to overwhelm the opponent. The synergy between the reanimation spells and powerful creatures creates a powerful and explosive gameplay experience.

The deck focuses on using deception and manipulation to quickly bring powerful creatures back from the graveyard to the battlefield. By utilizing various combo pieces and reanimation spells, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with high-impact creatures like Superior Spider-Man and Harvester of Misery. The deck also includes cards that provide card advantage and disruption to control the game state. With a mix of control and aggressive strategies, this combo-reanimator deck aims to outpace and outvalue the opponent to secure victory.

The deck focuses on using Deceit to cheat out powerful creatures like Superior Spider-Man and Doomsday Excruciator from the graveyard. It also utilizes cards like Requiting Hex and Bitter Triumph to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategies. The inclusion of cards like Stock Up and Insatiable Avarice help to generate card advantage and maintain a strong presence on the battlefield. The deck aims to quickly assemble powerful creatures and overwhelm the opponent with a mix of reanimation and control elements.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing a combination of counterspells, removal spells, card draw, and land manipulation. The deck focuses on maintaining board control and hand advantage through cards like Mightform Harmonizer, Consult the Star Charts, and Stock Up. It also includes a variety of removal options such as Full Bore, Fire Magic, and Sear to deal with threats on the board. The mana base is designed to support the color requirements of the spells in the deck while also providing flexibility with land options like Ba Sing Se and Escape Tunnel.
